title: CAN Frame structure
text: The system shall define a Frame type carrying: arbitration ID (uint32), extended-ID flag, RTR flag, FD flag, BRS flag, and data payload.

title: Max data length query
text: The system shall provide max_data_len(fd) returning 8 for standard CAN and 64 for CAN FD.

title: Frame filter structure
text: The system shall define a Filter type with ID and Mask fields.

title: Filter match predicate
text: The system shall implement Filter::matches(f) returning true when (f.id & mask) == (id & mask).

title: Spec version constant
text: The system shall export kSpecVersion = "0.2" matching the RELAY spec version.

title: IBus interface
text: The system shall define an IBus abstract interface with send(), subscribe(), and close() methods.

title: RELAY bridge
text: The system shall provide to_message(), from_message(), and adapt() to bridge CAN frames with relay::Message and relay::INode.

title: Error sentinel aliases
text: The system shall alias relay error sentinels (ErrClosed, ErrNotConnected, ErrTimeout, ErrPayloadTooLarge) in the can namespace.

title: Validate standard ID
text: validate_frame() shall throw ErrInvalidFrame when a non-extended frame ID exceeds 0x7FF.

title: Validate extended ID
text: validate_frame() shall throw ErrInvalidFrame when an extended frame ID exceeds 0x1FFFFFFF.

title: Validate data length
text: validate_frame() shall throw ErrInvalidFrame when standard CAN data exceeds 8 bytes or CAN FD data exceeds 64 bytes.

title: Validate RTR data
text: validate_frame() shall throw ErrInvalidFrame when an RTR frame carries data.

title: Validate RTR+FD combination
text: validate_frame() shall throw ErrInvalidFrame when both RTR and FD flags are set.

title: Validate BRS requires FD
text: validate_frame() shall throw ErrInvalidFrame when BRS is set without FD.

title: Virtual bus creation
text: virt::Bus::create() shall return a non-null shared_ptr to an in-process broadcast bus.

title: Virtual bus broadcast send
text: A frame sent on the virtual bus shall be delivered to all matching subscribers including the sender.

title: Virtual bus subscribe
text: subscribe() shall return a channel that receives frames matching supplied filters; with empty filters all frames are delivered.

title: Virtual bus back-pressure
text: The virtual bus shall support DropNewest, DropOldest, and Block back-pressure policies per subscriber.

title: Virtual bus lifecycle
text: close() shall be idempotent; send/subscribe after close shall return ErrClosed; subscriber channels shall be closed when the bus closes.

title: ISO-TP configuration
text: Conn::create() shall accept a Config with TxID, RxID, ExtIDs, BlockSize, STmin, and timeout.

title: ISO-TP RxID subscription
text: Conn::create() shall subscribe for the configured RxID on the provided bus.

title: ISO-TP ExtIDs flag
text: The ISO-TP Conn shall use 29-bit extended IDs when cfg.ext_ids is true.

title: ISO-TP Conn type
text: Conn shall be an opaque connection type obtained from Conn::create().

title: ISO-TP single-frame send
text: send() shall transmit payloads of 1–7 bytes as a Single Frame (SF).

title: ISO-TP first frame send
text: send() shall transmit the first 6 bytes of multi-frame payloads as a First Frame (FF).

title: ISO-TP consecutive frame send
text: send() shall transmit remaining bytes as Consecutive Frames (CF) after receiving Flow Control.

title: ISO-TP payload limit
text: send() shall return ErrPayloadTooLarge for payloads exceeding 4095 bytes.

title: ISO-TP single-frame receive
text: recv() shall reassemble a Single Frame and return the payload.

title: ISO-TP first-frame receive
text: recv() shall detect a First Frame and send a Flow Control response.

title: ISO-TP consecutive-frame receive
text: recv() shall assemble all Consecutive Frames into the complete payload.

title: ISO-TP sequence number check
text: recv() shall verify Consecutive Frame sequence numbers and return an error on mismatch.

title: ISO-TP bus-closed handling
text: recv() shall return an error when the underlying bus channel closes.

title: J1939 ID decoding
text: decode_id() shall extract Priority, PGN, and Source Address from a 29-bit J1939 CAN ID.

title: J1939 PDU format distinction
text: decode_id() shall treat PF < 240 as peer-to-peer (PS is destination) and PF >= 240 as broadcast (PS is part of PGN).

title: J1939 Data Page bit
text: decode_id() shall include the Data Page bit in the PGN.

title: J1939 ID encoding
text: encode_id() shall build a 29-bit J1939 CAN ID from Priority, PGN, and Source Address.

title: J1939 Bus send
text: Bus::send() shall encode and transmit J1939 frames, using BAM TP for payloads > 8 bytes.

title: J1939 Bus subscribe
text: Bus::subscribe() shall deliver decoded J1939 frames filtered by PGN; with no PGNs all frames are delivered.

title: E2E Config
text: The E2E Config shall include DataID and SourceID fields.

title: E2E header size
text: The E2E header shall be exactly 10 bytes.

title: E2E error kinds
text: The system shall define E2EErrorKind values for CRCMismatch, SequenceGap, and HeaderTooShort.

title: Protector construction
text: Protector shall be constructible from Config and maintain a monotonically increasing sequence counter.

title: Protector protect()
text: protect() shall prepend the 10-byte E2E header to the payload and return the combined buffer.

title: Sequence counter increment
text: Each call to protect() shall increment the sequence counter by 1.

title: Receiver header length check
text: unwrap() shall throw E2EError(HeaderTooShort) when data is shorter than 10 bytes.

title: Receiver CRC check
text: unwrap() shall throw E2EError(CRCMismatch) when the CRC does not match.

title: Receiver sequence gap check
text: unwrap() shall throw E2EError(SequenceGap) when the sequence number is not exactly lastSeq+1.

title: Receiver sequential success
text: unwrap() shall succeed for a sequence of consecutive frames with correct CRC.

title: Receiver thread safety
text: Receiver::unwrap() shall be thread-safe (mutex-protected sequence counter).

title: DBC parse messages
text: parse() shall read BO_ lines and populate DB.messages with Message entries.

title: DBC byte order
text: The parser shall support both LittleEndian (Intel @1) and BigEndian (Motorola @0) signal byte orders.

title: DBC parse signals
text: parse() shall read SG_ lines and populate Message.signals with Signal entries.

title: DBC message fields
text: Each parsed Message shall include: ID, name, DLC, sender, and signals map.

title: Signal physical decode
text: Signal::decode() shall extract raw bits and apply factor/offset to return the physical value.

title: VAL_ value table
text: parse() shall read VAL_ lines and populate Signal.values with raw→label mappings.

title: DB decode
text: DB::decode() shall return all signal physical values for a given message ID and data.

title: Protocol type
text: The system shall define a Protocol integer enum with zero reserved.

title: Protocol constants
text: The system shall define Protocol constants CAN=1, DDS=2, LIN=3, MQTT=4, RCP=5, SOMEIP=6.

title: Protocol to_string
text: to_string(Protocol) shall return the canonical upper-case name.

title: Version type
text: The system shall define a Version triple with major, minor, patch fields.

title: Version to_string
text: Version::to_string() shall return "MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH".

title: Message type
text: The system shall define a Message envelope with protocol, version, id, payload, timestamp, seq, and meta fields.

title: Message seq and meta optional
text: seq and meta shall default to zero/empty and be omitted from serialization when not set.

title: ErrClosed sentinel
text: The system shall define ErrClosed() returning a distinct error_code for the closed state.

title: ErrNotConnected sentinel
text: The system shall define ErrNotConnected() for the not-connected state.

title: ErrTimeout sentinel
text: The system shall define ErrTimeout() for the timeout state.

title: ErrPayloadTooLarge sentinel
text: The system shall define ErrPayloadTooLarge() for the oversized payload state.

title: Error sentinel distinctness
text: All four error sentinels shall be distinct (no two shall compare equal).

title: INode interface
text: The system shall define INode with protocol(), send(), subscribe(), and close() methods.

title: ICaller interface
text: The system shall define ICaller extending INode with a synchronous call() method.

title: BackPressurePolicy enum
text: The system shall define BackPressurePolicy with DropNewest=0, DropOldest=1, Block=2.

title: SubscriberConfig type
text: The system shall define SubscriberConfig with chan_depth, back_pressure, event_id, and topic_name.

title: SubscriberOption factories
text: The system shall provide with_channel_depth(), with_back_pressure(), with_event_id(), and with_topic() option factories.

title: apply_options()
text: apply_options() shall apply each option in order to a zero-initialized SubscriberConfig and return it.

title: effective_depth()
text: SubscriberConfig::effective_depth(d) shall return chan_depth if > 0, otherwise d.

title: kSpecVersion constant
text: The system shall export kSpecVersion = "0.2".

title: HealthStatus enum
text: The system shall define HealthStatus with OK=0, Degraded=1, Down=2.

title: Health struct
text: The system shall define a Health struct with status and details fields.

title: IHealthProvider interface
text: The system shall define IHealthProvider with a health() method.

title: Metrics struct
text: The system shall define a Metrics struct with write_count, deliver_count, drop_count, bytes_written, bytes_delivered, and error_count.

title: IMetricsProvider interface
text: The system shall define IMetricsProvider with a metrics() method.

title: IDrainer interface
text: The system shall define IDrainer with close_with_drain(timeout) that waits for all deliveries before closing.

title: with_event_id option
text: with_event_id(id) shall set event_id in SubscriberConfig.

title: with_topic option
text: with_topic(name) shall set topic_name in SubscriberConfig.

title: parse_protocol()
text: parse_protocol() shall map a protocol name (case-insensitive) to its Protocol constant and throw on unknown names.

title: Frame ID bounds validation
text: The system shall reject any CAN frame whose arbitration ID exceeds the maximum for its addressing mode (0x7FF for standard, 0x1FFFFFFF for extended) before the frame is admitted to any bus or processing path.

title: Payload length validation
text: The system shall reject any CAN frame whose data length exceeds the maximum for its frame type (8 bytes for standard CAN, 64 bytes for CAN FD) before the frame is transmitted or delivered.

title: RTR+FD mutual exclusion
text: The system shall reject any frame with both RTR and FD flags set, as this combination is undefined and could be used to confuse frame parsers.

title: E2E payload integrity
text: The E2E Protector shall prepend a CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE integrity header so that any single-bit or burst corruption of the protected payload is detectable by the receiver.

title: E2E CRC rejection
text: The E2E Receiver shall reject any frame whose computed CRC does not match the header CRC, returning E2EError(CRCMismatch), and shall not deliver the payload to the application.

title: E2E replay / reorder detection
text: The E2E Receiver shall detect replayed or reordered frames by verifying that each incoming sequence counter is exactly lastSeq+1, returning E2EError(SequenceGap) on violation.

title: Subscription allowlist filtering
text: IBus::subscribe() shall accept a non-empty filter list; when provided, only frames whose ID satisfies at least one filter's (id & mask) == (filter.id & mask) predicate shall be delivered to that subscriber.

title: Bus flooding — channel depth cap
text: Each subscriber channel shall have a bounded capacity; the bus shall never allocate unbounded memory in response to a high frame rate, regardless of how many frames are produced.

title: Bus flooding — subscriber isolation
text: A slow or full subscriber using DropNewest or DropOldest back-pressure shall not delay or block frame delivery to other subscribers.

title: ISO-TP oversized payload rejection
text: The ISO-TP transport shall reject any send() request for a payload exceeding 4095 bytes, returning ErrPayloadTooLarge, preventing integer-overflow in frame-count calculations.

title: ISO-TP sequence integrity
text: The ISO-TP receiver shall verify the consecutive-frame sequence number on each CF and return an error on mismatch, preventing partial-payload delivery from interleaved or injected frames.

title: Closed-bus send rejection
text: Any attempt to send a frame on a closed bus shall return ErrClosed and shall not access subscriber state, preventing use-after-free on shutdown.

title: Non-blocking channel try_send
text: Chan<T>::try_send() shall return immediately with SendResult::Full when the channel is at capacity, without blocking or allocating memory, preventing a producer from being held by a slow consumer.

title: E2E header minimum length check
text: The E2E Receiver shall reject any buffer shorter than the 10-byte header with E2EError(HeaderTooShort), preventing out-of-bounds reads on truncated or injected frames.

title: DBC unknown message safety
text: DB::decode() shall return an empty map for an unrecognised message ID rather than asserting or accessing uninitialised state, preventing information disclosure from unknown frame IDs.
